claim: Agentic RAG has fundamental problems: 85% of effort goes to retrieval, task completion rates are 50-60%, outputs require human review, and latency is unpredictable
actionable: Consider compiled knowledge layers when building production agentic RAG systems to move reasoning from query time to ingestion time
source_title: Andrej Karpathy's Wiki Idea Was Just Shipped by Pinecone

claim: Compiled knowledge engines move expensive reasoning work from query time to ingestion/build time, reducing token usage from 49k-528k to 6k per query
actionable: Build compiled artifact layers that pre-synthesize answers to expected agent questions rather than doing it at query time
source_title: Andrej Karpathy's Wiki Idea Was Just Shipped by Pinecone

claim: Non-determinism in agentic RAG means the same question asked 10 times may trigger different retrieval strategies, tools, and sub-agents
actionable: Implement task-specific compiled knowledge layers that constrain agent behavior to deterministic retrieval paths
source_title: Andrej Karpathy's Wiki Idea Was Just Shipped by Pinecone

claim: Reasoning cannot compensate for poor retrieval - if underlying data sources are unstructured and unreliable, no amount of LLM intelligence will fix it
actionable: Focus on data quality and structure before investing in advanced retrieval strategies or reasoning models
source_title: Andrej Karpathy's Wiki Idea Was Just Shipped by Pinecone

claim: Karpathy's LLM Wiki uses persistent markdown files that accumulate and compound knowledge, avoiding rediscovery on each query
actionable: Create and maintain markdown-based knowledge bases that agents query instead of raw data, updating them as new information arrives
source_title: Andrej Karpathy's Wiki Idea Was Just Shipped by Pinecone

claim: Compiled knowledge layers work best for repeatable, known-in-advance task patterns and don't accommodate the long tail of unexpected questions well
actionable: Use compiled knowledge layers for well-defined task completion; keep agentic RAG as fallback for exploratory or novel questions
source_title: Andrej Karpathy's Wiki Idea Was Just Shipped by Pinecone

claim: Compiled artifact generation using coding agents and eval-driven iteration can be expensive because it runs in feedback loops when data changes
actionable: Plan for the cost of maintaining compiled knowledge layers as data changes; batch updates rather than continuous recompilation
source_title: Andrej Karpathy's Wiki Idea Was Just Shipped by Pinecone
